preview_text:
The model favors South Korea at 62%, with South Africa holding only a 13% chance of victory. The underdog's path to three points is narrow under this framework, though the draw probability sits at a substantial 25%.

The 62–25–13 spread suggests South Korea enters as a clear favorite, yet the model assigns the draw a meaningful role. The gap between South Korea's win probability and a South African victory is roughly 50 percentage points, indicating asymmetry; however, the 25% draw rate signals the match retains competitive texture and is unlikely to be a runaway result.

The sportsbook consensus diverges notably from the model. Books assign South Korea 47% (14 percentage points lower than the model) and South Africa 25% (12 points higher). The draw sits near parity between sources at 28–25%. This represents a material gap: the market is materially more bullish on South Africa and skeptical of a South Korea win than the statistical forecast. The dashboard does not adjudicate which view is correct.
